reforms.description: The Mexican government aims to give citizens opportunities to access knowledge and education through the intelligent use of technologies, including reforms to support the use of electronic and IT media.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving access to knowledge and education is expected to enhance human capital and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the reform is not a structural overhaul but rather an enhancement of existing educational opportunities.
reforms.source_quote: Give Mexicans opportunities to access knowledge, learning and education by means of an intelligent use of technologies.

reforms.description: The Mexican government proposed a new regulation for the electricity sector to create market conditions, allowing private producers to enter the generation market while maintaining state control over transmission and distribution.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating private sector entry into electricity generation is expected to enhance efficiency and innovation in the sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the electricity sector.
reforms.source_quote: The reform proposed by the previous administration in 1999... was not approved by Congress. In 2002, the current administration submitted a new proposal to Congress, which is less ambitious than the previous one.

reforms.description: The e-Mexico project aims to eliminate the digital divide in Mexico by expanding communication infrastructure and providing government services to rural communities, with a commitment to spend 663 million pesos on various subprojects.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in communication infrastructure is expected to enhance access to services and stimulate economic activity in underserved areas.
reforms.status_evidence: The project has been successfully implemented as planned.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a major investment in national infrastructure aimed at bridging the digital divide.
reforms.source_quote: To achieve the objectives of universal service and a greater coverage, a social fund to finance investment in infrastructure in poor areas was created in 2002.

reforms.description: The e-Mexico programme was introduced to enhance the diffusion of new technologies, particularly ICT, by developing the ICT industry and improving internet access for the population, especially SMEs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing ICT access and industry development is expected to foster innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been successfully implemented as planned.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the reform is not a structural overhaul but rather an enhancement of existing technology diffusion efforts.
reforms.source_quote: The e-Mexico programme already mentioned is an important initiative that can improve ICT diffusion via several subprogrammes designed to develop Mexico’s ICT industry and market.

reforms.description: The Mexican government has been promoting collective remittances through the 'Tres por uno' programme, which matches funds contributed by migrant groups with additional public finance to support community projects, and the 'My community' programme promotes the creation of enterprises co-financed by migrants and the state government in poor areas of Guanajuato, aiming to retain potential migrants.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Supporting community projects and enterprise creation is expected to stimulate local economies and reduce migration pressures.
reforms.status_evidence: Both programmes have been successfully implemented as planned.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, these reforms are not structural changes but rather enhancements to existing community support initiatives.
reforms.source_quote: The first initiative was the 'Two for one' (Dos por uno) programme launched in Zacatecas in 1993, in which the state government added two additional dollars for every dollar brought by migrants groups; since 1996, the programme has become 'Three for one' (Tres por uno).

reforms.description: Creation of SARE (Sistema de Apertura Rápida de Empresas), a new system to start a business within one day, aimed at simplifying the process of business registration.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Simplifying business registration is expected to encourage entrepreneurship and stimulate economic activity.
reforms.status_evidence: The system has been successfully implemented as planned.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, this reform is an administrative improvement rather than a structural change.
reforms.source_quote: Creation of SARE (Sistema de Apertura Rápida de Empresas), a new system to start a business within one day.
